title: koanophyllon iteophyllum
intro: Koanophyllon iteophyllum, commonly known as Guaco or Rauwolfia, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The therapeutic actions of Koanophyllon iteophyllum are attributed to its ability to modulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit sedative effects. The bioactive constituents present in the plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and other phenolic compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Koanophyllon iteophyllum can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain due to its high concentration of glycosides. Prolonged use can also lead to diarrhea, headaches, and fatigue, which may be accompanied by dizziness and lightheadedness. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can cause cardiac problems, including irregular heartbeat and low blood pressure. Additionally, excessive consumption may trigger allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and respiratory issues.
